response: a product designer and a hardware engineer are working together on a groundbreaking new device that combines advanced materials and innovative design principles. the device uses a special composite material with unique thermal and electrical properties. the designer has proposed a geometric shape for the device that optimizes both heat dissipation and electrical conductivity. the device is modeled as a three-dimensional shape that can be described by the following parametric equations: x(u, v) = a sin(u) cos(v), y(u, v) = a sin(u) sin(v), z(u, v) = b cos(u), where 0 ≤ u ≤ π and 0 ≤ v < 2π. given that the surface area of this shape must be exactly 1000 square units to ensure optimal heat dissipation, find the values of a and b that satisfy this condition. the surface area of the shape is calculated using the formula for the surface area of a parametric surface: 1000 = ∫∫ sqrt( (∂x/∂u × ∂y/∂v - ∂x/∂v × ∂y/∂u)^2 + (∂y/∂u × ∂z/∂v - ∂y/∂v × ∂z/∂u)^2 + (∂z/∂u × ∂x/∂v - ∂z/∂v × ∂x/∂u)^2 ) du dv. solving this equation will give the values of a and b. the electrical conductivity across the surface of the device is given by a function σ(x, y, z) = c(x² + y² + z²), where c is a constant. calculate the total electrical conductivity across the surface when c = 0.5 and the values of a and b found in part 1 are used. the total electrical conductivity is calculated by integrating σ over the surface of the shape. the result will depend on the values of a and b found in part 1. the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword the pen is mightier than sword
